asset_type: TaraHome technical blog article cover and library card
prompt: Create a clean editorial smart-home photograph for a technical homeowner article cover, landscape 3:2 composition with generous breathing room and a strong center-safe crop. Scene: a warm, lived-in contemporary living room at early evening, natural oak sideboard and linen textures, restrained dark-green accents in one cushion and a small ceramic planter. A real adult homeowner stands beside the sideboard, calmly checking a smartphone while looking toward an ordinary table lamp that is visibly switched on, suggesting the physical device works even though one app may not. Include a second phone resting naturally face-down on the oak surface to suggest two smart-home platforms without showing any interface. Natural window light mixed with warm lamp light, realistic photography, honest domestic details, subtle depth of field, premium but not staged, muted TaraHome palette of warm cream, oak, charcoal, and deep forest green. Do not show logos, branded or invented smart-home hardware, legible screen text, QR codes, icons, headline text, poster design, charts, diagrams, network lines, routing paths, decorative connection lines, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, or sci-fi effects. The output must look like a real editorial home photograph, not an AI diagram.
